system: translational and rotational kinetic energy in declared velocity coordinates

state_variables.name: omega
state_variables.meaning: angular velocity about a declared fixed axis

practical: tachometer, gyroscope, or rotary encoder angular velocity

control_variables.name: m
control_variables.meaning: mass associated with the translational coordinate

control_variables.name: I_axis
control_variables.meaning: moment of inertia about the declared fixed axis

regime_assumptions: mass is constant over the measurement regime
rotation is about the declared fixed principal axis
coordinates are velocity coordinates, not displacement coordinates
no restoring potential, damping, or modal frequency claim is made by this card
equation_hook: K(v, omega) = 1/2 m v^2 + 1/2 I_axis omega^2

units_and_dimensions.I_axis: kg m^2 = J (rad s^-1)^-2 when rad is treated as the declared angular coordinate unit
units_and_dimensions.H: block units by coordinate; mass and inertia entries are not stiffness entries

coordinate_note: This is kinetic metric geometry in velocity coordinates. The Hessian entries are mass and moment of inertia, not restoring stiffnesses; they must not be compared entry-by-entry with spring or LC storage Hessians.

module_object.description: diagonal mass/inertia metric induced by kinetic energy in the declared velocity coordinates

derivation_status: exact from the stated nonrelativistic kinetic energy after the velocity coordinates and rotation axis are declared

ceiling_or_reference.description: no hidden coordinate or ceiling is declared in this card

positive_scope: This card admits kinetic energy as an exact finite positive quadratic object while keeping metric geometry distinct from stiffness and frequency geometry.
boundary: It does not describe potentials, oscillation frequencies, damping, relativistic kinetic energy, gyroscopic coupling, or hidden-load structure.
failure_mode: The attachment fails if the coordinates are not velocity coordinates, the rotation axis is not declared, the inertia tensor is not positive on the active coordinate, or the metric is used as a stiffness Hessian.
next_measurement: Measure mass or moment of inertia independently, then compare kinetic energy changes against v^2 and omega^2 over the declared coordinate range.
